summary:
Intrauterine adhesions cause changes like reduced or absent menstruation; a lack of response to hormonal stimulation suggests significant involvement consistent with Asherman syndrome diagnosis. **(Note:** This summary was crafted based directly from Agent1’s initial detailed explanation and focuses only on critical aspects related to identifying features indicative of Asherman syndrome.) To ensure clarity within two concise statements while maintaining relevance:

---

Key feature for diagnosing Asherman syndrome includes abnormal uterine bleeding due to scar tissue formation inside uterus. Lack of normal hormone-induced period signals more extensive damage typical of advanced cases.
